observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Services Office or the Inventory Committee to exhaust efforts and measures to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s assets and submit the RPCPPE, among others, not later than January 31 of each year, in order for the accuracy and existence of PPE accounts to be verified in a timely manner.  

We also recommended that the General Services and the Accounting Offices cause the reconciliation of the results of actual physical count with the property and accounting records pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular          No. 2020-006. The discrepancies between the inventory reports and the accounting records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant adhere to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs for the proper recording and classification of transactions and accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the following proposed adjusting journal entries to reclassify the receivable accounts.

We recommended that the General Services Office (GSO) maintain a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses. We suggested that a repair history form be devised, indicating the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made, and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the COA, the agency, and others concerned. 

We further recommended and the General Services and Accounting Offices agreed that the repair and maintenance history/record be attached to the disbursement vouchers (DVs) for repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each finding and recommendation discussed in the CY 2019 Annual Audit Report (AAR) pertinent to their offices, in compliance with Section 99 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit a written explanation on the noted deficiency and direct the concerned personnel to compel the supplier to return the required creditable tax from the transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents and a written justification on the deficiencies noted regarding the travel of the municipal officials/personnel to the National Literacy Conference and Awards.
We further recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the 2015 PEI amounting to ₱2,186,450.00.
We recommended the refund of the excess amount of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 totaling ₱3,609,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Inventory Committee, spearheaded by the GSO Department Manager, to exhaust efforts and measures to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s Property, Plant, and Equipment and submit the RPCPPE, among others, not later than 
January 31 of each year, in order for the accuracy and existence of PPE accounts to be verified in a timely manner.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-07 dated April 19, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Compute the depreciation expense for CY 2021 for each noted PPE accounts and provide the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected balances in the financial statements; and
Make sure that all depreciable assets are depreciated every year.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-08 dated April 20, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the concerned MHO personnel agreed to refund the excess amount received. Otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.
We also recommended and the Municipality agreed to ensure that future disbursements similar to the transaction discussed are consistent with laws, issuances, rules, and regulations of the government for proper and lawful implementation.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-09 dated April 25, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned officials to submit proof of attendance on the previously declared days that they physically reported for work; otherwise, the overpayment should be refunded.
We also recommended and the Municipality agreed to make sure that all future disbursements and claims similar to the transaction discussed are supported with sufficient and relevant documents to establish their validity.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-2022-10 dated May 4, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare accurately bank reconciliation statements;
Review the reconciling items, secure appropriate supporting documents, and cause the immediate recording of the reconciling items; and
Analyze and verify prospectively discrepancies and errors and other reconciling items as they occur and subsequently prepare JEVs for the immediate recording of the corrections/adjustments in the general ledger so as to ensure accurate presentation of the Cash and Cash Equivalents and other affected accounts.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-12 dated May 12, 2022.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust measures in reconciling the Municipality’ record with that of the Iloilo Provincial Government and in determining the causes of the discrepancies. Upon reconciliation, proper adjustments should be made, if warranted.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-13 dated May 13, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned departments/offices to:
Plan effectively the priority development projects to be included in the AIP by considering their feasibility and necessity;
Ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF through proper prioritization and fast-tracking of the implementation of development projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the constituents; and
Analyze and reconcile the discrepancies in the amounts reflected in the SAAOB and the QRGPPA in totality and not only for those pertaining to the 20% DF.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-03 dated March 29, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the MDRRMO Department Manager and Municipal Budget Officer to:
Submit a written explanation on the charging of regular MOOE against the LDRRMF, subject to the Audit Team’s further evaluation; and
Discontinue use and allocation of LDRRMF for any regular MOOE of the MDRRMO.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-01 dated March 9, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for the transfer of the unutilized balance for CY 2015 of ₱4.133 million from the Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, pursuant to Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to ensure henceforth that any unutilized amount of the LDRRMF be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the GF at the lapse of five years.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-06 dated April 11, 2022.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the list of PPAs chargeable against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years for transparency and efficient monitoring of funds.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-06 dated April 11, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Provide the Audit Team with an explanation on the noted discrepancy;
Make the necessary adjustments thereafter, if warranted; and
Review the records pertaining to LDRRMF regularly and analyze and resolve any discrepancies noted in a timely manner.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to see to it that the transfer to the STF in the Trust Fund books be made at year end in order for the unexpended balances to be incorporated in the ensuing year’s LDRRMFIP and be recognized properly in the Statement of Financial Performance.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-06 dated April 11, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the concerned officials and employees to cause the immediate liquidation of their unliquidated cash advances. Otherwise, any amount due to the concerned employees, whose cash advances have been outstanding for more than two months or 60 days already, should be withheld.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to refrain from allowing the granting of another cash advance to any official or employee with pending settlement or liquidation.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-11 dated May 11, 2022.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to set up and maintain complete subsidiary records for each special account and prepare accordingly the trial balance and financial statements for each special account in the General Fund at the end of each year.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-02 dated March 9, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the concerned department heads and personnel to give an update on the status on each audit observation and recommendation pertinent to their offices to the Municipal Accountant; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to seek assistance from other offices or within her office in consolidating the statuses of the audit observation and recommendation in the CY 2020 Annual Audit Report, and submit, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, the duly accomplished AAPSI within the prescribed period, in compliance with Section 91 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2021.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-04 dated April 5, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record transactions properly by exercising vigilance in the determination of the specific accounts to be used as provided for in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and avoid indiscriminate use of the Other Maintenance and Operating Expense account;
Capitalize costs that are directly attributable to Land or any PPE account as required in IPSAS 17; and
Prepare an adjusting entry to correct the understatement of Land account amounting to ₱0.969 million.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-05 dated April 8, 2022.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all future donations during the implementation of community quarantine or state of calamity, whether in cash or in kind, are properly received/acknowledged, recorded, accounted for, utilized, distributed, and reported without necessarily disregarding basic controls and issuances governing donations or aids during a disaster or pandemic.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the concerned officials and employees to cause the immediate liquidation of their unliquidated cash advances.


We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from allowing the granting of another cash advance to any official or employee with pending settlement or liquidation.


We further recommended that demand letters be sent to the concerned employees especially to those whose cash advances have been outstanding for more than two months already.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial statements on or before the fourteenth day of February so that review of the said statements and rendering of opinion on their fairness could be done on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in order to reflect the correct amounts of receivables for CY 2019 and to set up the RPT and SET Receivables at net amounts at the beginning of the year for years hereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance collection efforts so that RPT and SET Receivables are realized, and tax delinquencies are collected; and

Direct the Municipal Assessor to conduct actual inspection and inventory of real properties in the Municipality so that a proper classification, appraisal and assessment can be made, and a more attainable target of RPT and SET Receivables can be set.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Market Supervisor to strategize more effective measures to collect monthly rentals from market stallholders and impose strictly the penalties for late or non-payment thereof. 

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a contract of lease with all the stallholders to support the leasehold rights of both parties.

We also recommended that the Market Supervisor revisit the specific conditions for rental fees stated in the contract and ensure their proper implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to reconcile the Land account and maintain subsidiary ledger in order to ensure reliability of account as presented in the financial statements.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned personnel to exert efforts to make the necessary research for the retrieval of documents supporting the untitled parcels of land.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to:

Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of trust funds received from Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized funds.

Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the Source Agencies and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.
We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees refund the review fee of eighteen (18) personnel in the total amount of ₱12,600.00.
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the Medical Officer V refund the excess amount of Hazard Allowance totaling ₱68,469.00.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   Inventory
Committee to exhaust efforts and measures in the completion of the  physical
inventory count of the PPE and submit the RPCPPE not later than  January  31
of each year. The RPCPPE should  be  reconciled  with  the  records  of  the
Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Prepare and submit immediately the bank  reconciliation  statements  from
   January to December 2019 for all funds;


b. Effect adjusting/correcting entries, if necessary; and


c. Submit the same, on a monthly basis, to the Auditor’s  Office  for  audit
   purposes within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in order to  reflect  the
correct amounts of receivables for CY 2019 and to set up  the  RPT  and  SET
Receivables  at  net  amounts  at  the  beginning  of  the  year  for  years
hereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  concerned  officials
and employees to comply strictly with all policy guidelines  and  procedures
in  the  implementation  of  programs  funded  through  national  government
assistance so that  intended  beneficiaries  could  benefit  from  the  said
projects and to avoid disqualification from future projects or assistance.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  concerned  officials
and employees to prioritize  and  expedite  the  implementation  of  LGSF  –
Priority Programs and Projects in order to provide  the  essential  benefits
to the  indigent  individuals  and  families  and  to  achieve  the  overall
objective of the program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  Municipal  Budget
Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that approved SB  Resolutions
are secured prior to processing of payments for projects appropriated  under
programs/projects/activities stated in generic terms in  the  Annual  Budget
and Annual Procurement Plan.

We also recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  require  the  Municipal
Development Council and  concerned  offices  to  be  more  specific  in  the
identification of priority projects and programs to be charged  against  the
20% MDF such that the projects included in the APP  and  the  Annual  Budget
already contain or provide sufficient details and costing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct  the  LDRRMO  and
OIC - Municipal  Accountant  to  adhere  strictly  with  the  provisions  of
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, specifically in  the  submission
of Monthly Report on Sources  and  Utilization  of  LDRRMF  so  that  proper
utilization, recording, and releasing of the fund could  be  verified  on  a
timely manner and that proper charging and recording could be effected.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  OIC  -  MDRRMO  to
utilize the 70% of the LDRRMF for  projects  and  activities  enumerated  in
NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.  Subsequent transactions with  similar
deviations shall be disallowed in audit. For other operational needs of  the
MDRRMO, adequate funds from the general fund should be appropriated for  the
purpose.

We  recommended  that  the  OIC  -   Municipal   Accountant   transfer   all
unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and the DRRM Fund - MOOE  to  the
Trust Liability - DRRM in the Trust Fund books, pursuant to  Section  5.1.10
of COA Circular No. 2012-002, to mitigate the risk of misuse of the funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance collection efforts so that  RPT
   and SET Receivables are realized, and tax  delinquencies  are  collected;
   and


b. Direct the Municipal Assessor to conduct actual inspection and  inventory
   of real properties in the Municipality so that a  proper  classification,
   appraisal and assessment can be made, and a more attainable target of RPT
   and SET Receivables can be set.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
direct the Inventory
Committee to exert more
effort in the
completion of the
physical inventory of
the PPE and submit the
RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of each
year.

We further recommended
that the General
Services Officer
prepare the property
cards, reconcile the
postings with the
records of the
Municipal Accountant
and effect the
necessary adjustments
accordingly.

We recommended that the
OIC-Municipal
Accountant make the
following the adjusting
entry to record the
cost of Ambulance unit:

Dr - Motor Vehicles
Cr - Government Equity
Amount: P858,000.00

We also recommended
that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the
concerned personnel to
fast track the transfer
of ownership of the
Ambulance unit from the
PCSO to the
Municipality of
Tigbauan, Iloilo.

We recommended that the
LDRRMO and
OIC-Municipal
Accountant adhere
strictly with the

No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012
specifically in the

Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF so
that the proper
utilization, recording
and releases of the
fund can be verified in
a timely manner and
that proper charging
and recording can be
effected.

We also recommended
that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the
LDRRMO to ensure
optimum utilization of
the LDRRMF through
proper planning,
prioritization and
fast-tracking the
implementation of
priority projects,
programs and activities
to afford its
constituents the
readiness of resources
in times of disaster.

We also recommended
that the LDRRMO
strictly follow the
guidelines set in
Section 6.5 of
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-1 dated March
25, 2013 so that the
release and utilization
of funds will be set
forth in the revised AIP
and will be in
accordance with the
Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management
Plan of the
municipality.

We further recommended
that the OIC-Municipal
Accountant transfer all
unexpended/ unobligated
balance of the QRF and
the DRRMF-MOOE to the
Trust Liability-DRRM in
the Trust Fund books
pursuant to Section
5.1.10 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 to lessen
the risk of misuse of
fund.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive,
Sangguniang Bayan and
officials concerned
appropriate one percent
(1%) of its Internal
Revenue Allotment in
the Annual Budget for
the strengthening and
implementation of
programs, projects and
activities of Local
Council for the
Protection of Children
(LCPC) in order to
cater the needs and
uphold the rights and
interests of the
children in the
municipality.

We further recommended
that the Local Chief
Executive direct the
Members of Municipal
Council for the
Protection of Children
(MCPC) to prepare and
submit the Local
Investment Plan for
Children (LIPC) and
Local Development Plan
for Children (LDPC).

We further recommended
that the concerned
Municipal Officials and
Employees cause the
refund of the 2015 PEI
amounting to
P2,186,450.00.

We recommended that
concerned municipal
officials and employees
refund the review fee of
eighteen (18) personnel
in the total amount of
P 12,600.00.

We recommended the
refund of the excess
amount of Productivity
Enhancement Incentive
for CY 2014 totaling P
3,609,000.00 paid to
officials and
personnel.

We recommended that all concerned municipal officials  and  employees  cause
the immediate liquidation of cash advances in  accordance  with  the  above-
cited provisions of law.

We also recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant:


a. Send within ten (10) days before the  expiration  of  the  30-day  period
   specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a  written
   reminder under  signature  of  the  head  of  agency  or  his  authorized
   representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate
   his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant  to  Section  3.3.2  (a)  of  COA
   Circular 96-004.


b. Remove the name of the official or employee from the subsequent  payrolls
   until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated,  if  the
   official/ employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash  advance  within
   the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.3.2(b) of the same circular.



c. Send a demand letter to the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer to settle  his
   outstanding cash advances as soon as possible.

d. Refrain  from  granting  additional  cash  advance  to  any  official  or
   employee unless the previous cash  advance  given  to  him/her  is  first
   settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   Inventory
Committee to exert efforts in the completion of the  physical  inventory  of
the PPE and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.


We further  recommended  that  the  General  Services  Officer  prepare  the
property cards, reconcile the postings with the  records  of  the  Municipal
Accountant and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  instruct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to reconcile  the  Land
account and maintain subsidiary ledger in order  to  ensure  reliability  of
account as presented in the financial statements.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  concerned
personnel to exert efforts to make the necessary research for the  retrieval
of documents supporting the untitled parcels of land.

We  recommended  that  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  make  the   following
adjusting entry to record the cost of ambulance unit:

|Motor Vehicles           |P 858,000.00         |                 |
|Government Equity        |                     |P 858,000.00     |
|To record the receipt of ambulance unit donated by PCSO          |


We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct  the  concerned
personnel to fast track the transfer of  ownership  of  the  Ambulance  unit
from the PCSO to the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  instruct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions stated under COA  Circular
No. 97-002 in granting cash advances to any  official  or  employee  and  to
ensure that the transactions are duly supported with complete  documentation
before processing the claims.

We  further  recommended  that  succeeding  payments  for   future   similar
transactions should be made through check directly issued to the  payee  and
that liquidation thereof will be submitted for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  OIC-  Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of trust funds received  from
   Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized funds.

b. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the  Source  Agencies  and
   prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  instruct   the   Market
Supervisor to initiate effective measures to collect  monthly  rentals  from
market stallholders and strictly impose  the  penalties  for  late  or  non-
payment thereof. Demand letters should be issued to delinquent  stallholders
to formally  remind  them  of  their  dues  with  the  municipality  and  to
institute legal sanctions, if warranted.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a contract  of
lease with all the stallholders to support  the  leasehold  rights  of  both
parties.

We  also  recommended  that  the  Market  Supervisor  revisit  the  specific
conditions of the contract and to apply the increase on  the  monthly  rental
fee prospectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  LDRRMO  and  OIC-
Municipal Accountant to adhere  strictly  with  the  provisions  of  Section
5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically  in
the submission of monthly Report on Sources  and  Utilization  of  DRRMF  so
that proper utilization,  recording  and  releases  of the  fund  could  be
verified on a timely manner and that proper charging and  recording  can  be
effected.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRMO to:

1. To properly program the LDRRMFIP for efficient and effective  utilization
   of LDRRMF in conformity with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM  and  DILG  JMC  No.
   2013-1.

2. To ensure that the release and  utilization  of  the  30%  QRF  shall  be
   supported by a resolution of the local sanggunian declaring the LGU under
   state of calamity.

3. To ensure optimum utilization of  the  LDRRMF  through  proper  planning,
   prioritization and fast-tracking the implementation of priority projects,
   programs and activities to  afford  its  constituents  the  readiness  of
   resources in times of disaster.

4. To follow the guidelines set in Section 6.5 of NDRRMC, DBM and  DILG  JMC
   No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 so that the release  and  utilization  of
   unexpended LDRRMF will be set forth in the revised AIP  and  will  be  in
   accordance with the LDRRMP of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the  BAC  Members  to
strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184  on  all
procurement activities of the municipality.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to adhere with the provisions stated under COA Circular  No.  97-
002 in granting cash advances to any official or employee and to  make  sure
that the transactions are duly supported with complete documentation  before
processing the claims. Stop  the  practice  of  paying  purchases  of  goods
through cash advance rather  should  be  paid  by  check  directly  to  the
suppliers and withhold the required taxes from payments thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit a  written  explanation
on the noted deficiency and direct the concerned  personnel  to  compel  the
supplier to return the required creditable tax from the transaction.

We also recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
personnel to strictly adhere with the provisions of the Revised IRR of  R.A.
No. 9184 on all procurement activities of the municipality.  Future  similar
deviation  from  the  procurement  laws,  rules  and  regulations  will   be
disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant  to  withhold  the  10%  retention  money  on  all  payments   of
infrastructure  projects  to  serve  as  warranty  against  hidden  defects,
pursuant to Section 6.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We  also  recommended  that  the  Engineering  Office  coordinate  with  the
Accounting Office to reconcile the records on completed projects.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to process DVs / claim on the advance payment / mobilization  fee
only upon submission to and acceptance of an irrevocable standby  letter  of
credit, bank guarantee or a surety bond as prescribed under Section  4.2  of
Annex E of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to  require  all  employees/personnel  to  attach  taxi  metered
receipts to support claims for travel with taxi fare of more than P75.00 as
required under COA Circular No. 96-005 and Paragraph 27 of LTFRB Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-004.

We also recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  require  all  immediate
supervisors to make sure that itineraries of travel be  properly  filled  up
before approving such.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
personnel to submit the lacking documents and a  written  justification  on
the  deficiencies   noted   regarding   the   travel   of   the   municipal
officials/personnel to the National Literacy Conference and Awards.

We also recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
personnel to refund the excessive claim on incidental expenses amounting to
P16,000.00.

We further  recommended  that  the  department  heads  properly  assess  the
necessity of every travel of official/personnel  before  approving  such  to
avoid unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit
all collections to the
authorized depository
bank daily or not later
than the next banking
day as prescribed in
the provisions of
Section 69 of PD 1445
and Section 32 of MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
direct the
OIC-Municipal
Accountant to
coordinate with the
concerned depository
bank to validate the
identified reconciling
items and to draw the
appropriate journal
entries to take up all
reconciling items and
adjustments of errors
affecting Cash in Bank
and other related
accounts.

We recommended that the
OIC-Municipal

We recommended that the
Municipal Health
Officer and the Medical
Officer V refund the
excess amount of
HazardAllowance for CY
2015 totaling P68,469.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Reclassify the fund transfer from Subsidy to Other Local Government Units to Due from other LGUs; Monitor and demand the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs; Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the MPDO submit copy of the agreed policy and procedures in the monitoring of PAPs and the corresponding report thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: Transfer the fund from General Fund to Trust Fund; Adhere strictly with the provisions of General Appropriations Act of 2016, COA Circular No. 94-013 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan revisit Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 and Executive Order No. 181 and should refrain from incurring additional PS expenses/cost until such time that the limitation is observed. The implementation of any increase in PS cost should be held in abeyance until the municipality shall not exceed PS limitation/cap.
We further recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the 2015 PEI amounting to P2,186,450.00.

We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees refund the review fee of eighteen (18) personnel in the total amount of P12,600.00.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Officials concerned see to it that payments for disbursements should be adhered to established rules, regulations, procedural guidelines, policies, principles or practices that have gained recognition in law.

We recommended the refund of the excess amount of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 totaling P3,609,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Officials concerned see to it that payments for allowances and other personnel benefits should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing such payments to avoid the suspension and disallowance of the amount.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   Municipal
Treasurer to deposit all  collections  to  the  authorized  depository  bank
daily or  not  later  than  the  next  banking  day  as  prescribed  in  the
provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445  and  Section  32  of  MNGAS  for  LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the concerned depository bank to validate  the
identified reconciling items and to draw the appropriate journal entries  to
take up all reconciling items and adjustments of errors  affecting  Cash  in
Bank and other related accounts.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:

1. Make journal entries to reclassify the fund transfer  from  General  Fund
   to Trust Fund;
2. Adhere strictly with the provisions  of  General  Appropriations  Act  of
   2017, COA Circular  No.  94-013  and  other  pertinent  laws,  rules  and
   regulations.


We also recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
municipal employees to fast  track  the  implementation  of  the  identified
projects  and  programs  that  will  cater  the  needs   of   the   targeted
beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to:


1. Make the following adjusting entries:


        |Due from NGAs       |501,500.00          |                    |
|Prior Period        |                    |501,000.00          |
|Adjustment          |                    |                    |
|To record the fund transfer to Tigbauan NHS                   |


2. Monitor and demand the liquidation reports from the recipient –  Tigbauan
   NHS;


3. Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular  No.  2012-001  and
   other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal  Engineer
and other concerned personnel  to  fast  track  the  implementation  of  the
priority projects and programs in order  to  provide  the  benefits  to  the
targeted beneficiaries and to serve the overall objective of the ADM.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  all   concerned
Municipal Officials and Employees to  cause  the  immediate  liquidation  of
cash advances in accordance with the above-cited provisions of law.


We also recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant:


a. Send within ten (10) days before the expiration  of  the  30  day  period
   specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a  written
   reminder under  signature  of  the  head  of  agency  or  his  authorized
   representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate
   his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant  to  Section  3.3.2  (a)  of  COA
   Circular 96-004.
b. Remove the name of the official or employee from the subsequent  payrolls
   until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated,  if  the
   official/ employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash  advance  within
   the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.3.2(b) of the same circular.



c. Send a demand letter to the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer to settle  his
   outstanding cash advances as soon as possible.

d. Refrain  from  granting  additional  cash  advance  to  any  official  or
   employee unless the previous cash  advance  given  to  him/her  is  first
   settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   Inventory
Committee to exert more effort in the completion of the  physical  inventory
of the PPE and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.


We further  recommended  that  the  General  Services  Officer  prepare  the
property cards, reconcile the postings with the  records  of  the  Municipal
Accountant and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant provide  the  corresponding
allowance for depreciation  for  properties  valued  at  P143,550,940.92  in
compliance to existing government rules and regulations.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal  Accountant  make  the  following  the
adjusting entry to record the cost of Ambulance unit:

|Motor Vehicles           |P 858,000.00         |                 |
|Government Equity        |                     |P 858,000.00     |
|To record the receipt of Ambulance unit donated by PCSO          |


We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct  the  concerned
personnel to fast track the transfer of  ownership  of  the  Ambulance  unit
from the PCSO to the Municipality of Tigbauan, Iloilo.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on time in order to check the accuracy of the balances of the bank accounts and to submit the journal vouchers to ensure proper recording of all valid reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the GL. Any discrepancy between the bank statements and the agency records should be immediately reconciled and adjusted or be brought to the attention of the depository bank for timely correction and/or confirmation.
Make a formal request to the depository bank for the release of bank statements with corresponding debit/credit advices within the prescribed regulations.
Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer of the Cash in Bank balances per books and per cashbooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of PPE and reconcile the result of physical count with the property and accounting records and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.
We also recommended that Municipal Mayor create a committee on PPE appraisal who in turn shall conduct appraisal of all acquired properties in prior years including donated property for valuation purposes.
We further recommended that the GSO and the Municipal Accountant prepare the property cards and ledger cards, respectively and to reconcile the postings/records of their PPE cards thereon.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was created however, physical inventory of all properties as of December 31, 2017 was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the corresponding allowance for depreciation for properties valued at P144,779,448.15 in compliance to existing government rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Not all PPE were provided with allowance for depreciation. Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the above-cited provisions of law.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 day period specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant to Section 3.3.2 (a) of COA Circular 96-004 dated April 19, 1996. Refrain from granting additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Remove the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated, if the official/ employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.3.2(b) of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances amounting to P1,593,837.33 remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking certificate or document approved by the Secretary of Health to justify the legality of claims for Hazard Pay in accordance with Section 3.0, Item 3.1 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2016, and Section 21 of RA 7305.
We further recommended the refund of the excess payment of Hazard Pay amounting to P3,332.11 from the concerned employees and for the municipality to strictly comply with Section 3, Item 3.3 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the three (3) Staff positions to assist the LDRRMO following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6 Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff. We also recommended that the LDRRMO Designate and OIC Municipal Accountant adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically in the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF so that the proper utilization, recording and releases of the fund can be verified in a timely manner and that proper charging and recording can be effected.
We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant transfer all unexpended/ unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Liablility-DRRM in the Trust Fund books pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to lessen the risk of misuse of fund.
We also recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liability – DRRM account to determine the sources as to which year or who donated the fund pursuant to Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to identify the amount that can be transferred from Special Trust Fund to General Fund for Social Services at the lapse of five years.
We further recommended that the LDRRMO Designate strictly follow the guidelines set in Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 so that the release and utilization of funds will be set forth in the revised AIP and will be in accordance with the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to prioritize the concretization of the road going to the proposed Eco-Park to fast track the utilization of the site as recommended in NSWMC Resolution No. 26 Series of 2009 and for the proper rehabilitation of the closed dumpsite to take place in compliance with Part III Rule VII Section 3(h) of the IRR of RA 9003.
We further recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board formulate and adopt revenue-generating measures pursuant to Section 12 (4) of RA 9003 in order to fund the expenses and projects related to Solid Waste Management. We also recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in charging expenditures against 20% Development Fund and to formulate projects, programs and activities that partake the nature of capital investment and has long term benefits to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: It is still being used as disposal site for residual waste. Construction of proposed Eco-park is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point System to comply with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 as updated by JMC 2016-01 and particularly on the procedures to be followed in the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of agency annual GAD Plans and Budget and GAD AR in order to mainstream gender perspectives in their policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay as prescribed under the GAA of 2016 effective January 2017 and onwards.

We recommended that the Municipal Administrator/Municipal Engineer: Submit regularly to the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel and lubricants consumed during the period and to effectively control fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures and avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant discontinue the processing of claims for gasoline, oil and lubricants unless these are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer and the Medical Officer V to refund the excess amount of Hazard Allowance for CY 2015 totaling P68,469.00.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that payments for allowances and other personnel benefits should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing such payments to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started to deduct from the salaries of the officials concerned the amounts pertaining to the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the refund of the excess amount of Productivity           Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 totaling P3,609,000.00 paid to           officials and personnel. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor and           Municipal Officials concerned see to it that payments for allowances and           other personnel benefits should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing           such   payments to avoid the suspension and disallowance of the amount. The           aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit           Observation Memorandum No. 2015-001-100(14) dated February 19, 2015.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide every property and equipment with corresponding allowance for depreciation based on the formula as provided for by existing rules and regulations to reflect the true and correct balance of the account in the financial statements. The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2015-003-100(14) dated February 19, 2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. Depreciation will be taken up after the inventory of PPE has been reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan revisit Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 and Executive Order No. 181 and should refrain from incurring additional PS expenses/cost until such time that the limitation is observed. The implementation of any increase in PS cost should be held in abeyance until the municipality shall not exceed PS limitation/cap. We further recommend that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the 2015 PEI amounting to P2,186,450.00.

We recommend that concerned municipal officials and employees refund the review fee of eighteen (18) personnel in the total amount of P 12,600.00. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Officials concerned see to it that payments for disbursements should be adhered to established rules, regulations, procedural guidelines, policies, principles or practices that have gained recognition in law.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRM Officer to adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012 by submitting regularly to the COA Audit Team of the Municipality the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Municipal Development Council. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant include a summary of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to the Financial Statements as presented in Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. The LDRRM Officer is newly designated and is not aware of the reports to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer and the Medical Officer V refund the excess amount of Hazard Allowance for CY 2015 totaling P 68,469.00. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to see it that payments for allowances and other personnel benefits should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing such payments to avoid suspensions/ disallowances in audit.
observations.reason: The Municipality has started to deduct from the salaries of the officials concerned the amounts pertaining to the disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations


We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

1. Prepare and submit monthly Bank  Reconciliation  Statements  on  time  in
   order to check the accuracy of the balances of the bank accounts  and  to
   submit the journal entry vouchers to ensure proper recording of all valid
   reconciling items that  require  adjustment/correction  in  the  GL.  Any
   discrepancy between the bank statements and  the  agency  records  to be
   immediately reconciled and adjusted or be brought to the attention of the
   depository bank for timely correction and/or confirmation.


2. Make a formal request to the depository bank  for  the  release  of  bank
   statements with corresponding debit/credit advices within the  prescribed
   period.


3. Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer of the  Cash
   in Bank balances per books and per cashbooks.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory  Committee
to conduct actual physical inventory of PPE  and  reconcile  the  result  of
physical count with the property  and  accounting  records  and  submit  the
RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.


We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive  create  a  committee  on
PPE  appraisal  who  in  turn  shall  conduct  appraisal  of  all   acquired
properties  in  prior  years  including  donated  property   for   valuation
purposes.


We further recommended that the GSO and  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare
the property cards and ledger  cards,  respectively  and  to  reconcile  the
postings/records of their PPE cards thereon.

Recommendation


We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  provide  the  corresponding
allowance for depreciation  for  properties  valued  at  P144,779,448.15  in
compliance to existing government rules and regulations.

Recommendations


We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


1. Reclassify the fund transfer  from  Subsidy  to  Other  Local  Government
   Units to Due from other LGUs;
2. Monitor and demand the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs;
3. Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular  No.  2012-001  and
   other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the MPDO submit  copy  of  the  agreed  policy  and
procedures in the monitoring of PAPs and the corresponding report thereon.

Recommendations


We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:

1. Transfer the fund from General Fund to Trust Fund;
2. Adhere strictly with the provisions  of  General  Appropriations  Act  of
   2016, COA Circular  No.  94-013  and  other  pertinent  laws,  rules  and
   regulations.

Recommendations

We recommended that all concerned Municipal Officials  and  Employees  cause
the immediate liquidation of cash advances in  accordance  with  the  above-
cited provisions of law.


We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


a. Send within ten (10) days before the expiration  of  the  30  day  period
   specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a  written
   reminder under  signature  of  the  head  of  agency  or  his  authorized
   representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate
   his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant  to  Section  3.3.2  (a)  of  COA
   Circular 96-004 dated April 19, 1996. Refrain  from  granting  additional
   cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance
   given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.



b. Remove the name of the official or employee from the subsequent  payrolls
   until such time that the travel cash advance has been  fully  liquidated,
   if the official/ employee concerned fails to liquidate the  cash  advance
   within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.3.2(b)  of  the  same
   circular.

Recommendations


We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking certificate or  document  approved  by  the
Secretary of Health to justify the legality of  claims  for  Hazard  Pay  in
accordance with Section 3.0, Item 3.1  of  DBM-DOH  Joint  Circular  No.  1,
series of 2016, and Section 21 of RA 7305.


We further recommended the refund  of  the  excess  payment  of  Hazard  Pay
amounting  to  P3,332.11  from  the  concerned   employees   and   for   the
municipality to strictly comply with Section 3, Item 3.3  of  DBM-DOH  Joint
Circular No. 1, series of 2016.

Recommendations


We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Sangguniang  Bayan
hire qualified personnel to occupy the three (3) Staff positions  to  assist
the LDRRMO following the position titles and salary grades  set  by  Rule  6
Section 2 of IRR of RA No.  10121  and  Item  5.2,  5.3  and  5.4  of  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be  able  to  properly
perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff.  We
also recommended that the LDRRMO will be an officially appointed person  and
not a designated one so that the functions and duties can  be  performed  to
its  maximum  and  conflict  in  terms  of  time,  attention  and  focus  in
performing the duties and  functions  between  the  official  item  and  the
designated position can be prevented.

We recommended that  the  LDRRMO  Designate  and  OIC  Municipal  Accountant
adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 5.1.5  of  COA  Circular  No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically in the submission of  monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF so that the  proper  utilization,
recording and releases of the fund can be verified in a  timely  manner  and
that proper charging and recording can be effected.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant  transfer  all  unexpended/
unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the  Trust  Liablility-
DRRM in the Trust Fund books pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular  No.
2012-002 to lessen the risk of misuse of fund.

We also recommended that the OIC Municipal  Accountant  maintain  subsidiary
ledgers for the Trust Liability – DRRM account to determine the  sources  as
to which year or who donated the fund pursuant  to  Section  5.1.12  of  COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and to identify the amount  that  can  be  transferred
from Special Trust Fund to General Fund for Social Services at the lapse  of
five years.

We further  recommended  that  the  LDRRMO  Designate  strictly  follow  the
guidelines set in Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint  Memorandum  Circular
No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 so  that  the  release  and  utilization  of
funds will be set forth in the revised AIP and will be  in  accordance  with
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan of the municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory committee to conduct an actual physical count of property, plant and equipment, as well as, the inventory accounts to ascertain their existence, and prepare a report thereon. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the account with the accounting records in order to establish the correctness of the Property, Plant and Equipment and the inventory accounts.
observations.reason: InventoryCommittee is to begin the inventory of PPE upon appointment of the Supply Officer to take charge of the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant strictly comply with Section 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011. Any discrepancy between the bank statements and the agency records should be immediately reconciled and adjusted or be brought to the attention of the depository bank for timely correction and/or confirmation. The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2015-004-(100,200,300)(14) dated February 19, 2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. The Municipal Accountant is working on the Bank Reconciliations and is now until the beginning of 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances. Cash advances for travel should be liquidated within thirty (30) days after completion of the said travel. We also recommend the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to withhold the salaries of all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted. The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2015-005-100(14) dated February 19, 2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. The Municipal Accountant has already sent out letters to the officers concerned and is awaiting reply.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality should fully implement the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund in order that the constituents will enjoy optimum benefits derived out of these projects and to enhance the general welfare of the populace. The Annual Development/Investment Plan must be drawn in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Council who shall determine the nature of the programs/projects that should be prioritized so that accomplishment thereof will be made efficiently and effectively.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert all possible efforts to collect rentals from delinquent stallholders in order to increase its collections. Management should send out monthly Statement of Accounts to market stallholders reminding them of their monthly rentals. Demand letters should also be issued by the Municipal Treasurer to delinquent stallholders and necessary sanctions should be imposed on market stallholders who do not comply/adhere to the provisions as stipulated in the Market Code of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to implement strictly the provisions of COA Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. Strict adherence thereto will minimize and may eventually eliminate suspensions and disallowances during the conduct of post audit. Also, immediate corrective measures can be done for all deficiencies noted in the course of review, evaluation and inspection.

Require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and the Manual for Audit of Fuel Consumption prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61, dated September 26, 1977, on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. The Municipal Mayor issued an office order to the officials concerned to comply with the audit recommendation starting this quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Engineer exert effort to fully implement the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund in order that the constituents will enjoy optimum benefits derived out of these projects and to enhance the general welfare of the populace. The Annual Development/Investment Plan must be drawn in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Council who shall determine the nature of the programs/projects that should be prioritized so that accomplishment thereof will be made efficiently and effectively.
